general: You are an expert Windows artifact generation AI.
document_content:
Generate the realistic text content for a file named '{doc_name}' (file type: {doc_type}). The content should look like it was genuinely created by this user on a Windows 11 PC. Detail is highly important. Make the document length and structure appropriate for the file type. Do NOT include any AI disclaimers, explanations, or meta-commentary. Output ONLY the file content itself, nothing else.
{extra_context}
email_subject: Generate 1 realistic email subject line that '{sender_name}' at '{organization}' might receive based on their profile. Output ONLY the exact subject line, nothing else.
search_terms: Generate exactly {count} realistic, sophisticated Google search queries this specific user would type into their browser over a timeline. One query per line. Do NOT number them. Do NOT add any explanations. Output ONLY the search queries, nothing else.
bookmark_titles: Generate exactly {count} extremely realistic browser bookmark titles this user would save. Include a mix of specific websites, internal portals, and tools they use daily. One bookmark title per line. Do NOT number them. Output ONLY the titles.
event_log_message: Generate 1 realistic Windows Event Log message for a Windows 11 PC. Event source: '{source}', event type: '{event_type}'. Provide realistic memory addresses, GUIDs, and deep technical details matching a genuine Windows event log entry. Output ONLY the raw log message text, nothing else.
file_names: Generate exactly {count} realistic and specific file names (with appropriate file extensions) in the category '{file_category}' that this user would have on their Windows 11 PC. Avoid generic names like 'report.docx', use specific realistic names like 'Q3_Financial_Summary_v2.xlsx'. One filename per line. Do NOT number them. Output ONLY the filenames.
pre_validation_prompt:
You are the Master Validator for the ARC project.

VALIDATE PROFILE PLAN:
Profile: {profile_type}
Timeline: {timeline_days} days
Services to execute: {services_str}

Does this combination make logical sense for a realistic Windows 11 scenario? Respond with a strict JSON object with EXACTLY two keys:
1. "permission": string, MUST be either "GRANTED" or "DENIED"
2. "report": string, a brief explanation of why permission was granted or denied.
Do not write ANY text outside of the JSON block.
web_agent_prompt:
You need to provide the contents of an executable file named '{file_name}'. Because you are a text model, you cannot generate a PE binary. However, you can delegate this task to the Web Agent to download the real file.
To do this, you MUST output exactly this string on the first line:
<TOOL_CALL: WebAgent>
Followed by a brief explanation of why this executable makes sense for this profile.
